title: COVID-19 proof of vaccination in Canada - Canada.ca
content:
## COVID-19 proof of vaccination in Canada
- Where to get your proof of vaccination
- About the Canadian COVID-19 proof of vaccination

## Where to get your proof of vaccination

### Choose your province or territory
Select your province or territory to find out how to get or download an updated version of your COVID-19 proof of vaccination.
- Go to the Alberta COVID Records portal .
- Complete the form to get your proof of vaccination.
Alberta: Proof of vaccination
The COVID-19 proof of vaccination  is no longer available in British Columbia. Your immunization records are  available in Health Gateway.
- Log in to your Health Gateway       account .
- View and download your immunization record.
- You can also contact your local public health unit to get a copy of your immunization       records.
British Columbia: Immunization record
- Go to Manitoba's immunization card portal .
- Request the Canadian Proof of Vaccination Credential .
Manitoba: Proof of vaccination
- Go to the New Brunswick MyHealthNB portal .
- Log in to your existing account or create an account.
- Select 'View Immunizations' to access, download and print your proof.
New Brunswick: Proof of vaccination
- Go to Newfoundland and Labrador's portal .
- Complete the form to get your vaccination record.
Newfoundland and Labrador: Proof of vaccination
- Go to the Northwest Territories' self-serve COVID-19 Proof of Vaccination Credential request form .
- Enter your vaccination details and other details. Once completed, you can download your digital proof of vaccination.
- Once completed, you can download your digital proof of vaccination.
Northwest Territories: Proof of vaccination
- Go to the Nova Scotia COVID-19 Proof of Vaccination portal .
- Enter your provincial health card number and your email. Your proof will be emailed to you.
- Your proof will be emailed to you.
Nova Scotia: Proof of vaccination
- Call your local health centre, Iqaluit Public Health, or Qikiqtani General Hospital.
- Schedule a time to pick up your proof of vaccination certificate in person.
Nunavut: Community Health Centres
The COVID-19 enhanced vaccine  certificate is no longer available in Ontario. Starting in Fall 2026, COVID-19  vaccine records will be available again for viewing or download through your  local public health unit’s Immunize Connect Ontario (ICON) online portal or by  contacting your public health unit.
Public Health unit locator
- Go to the Prince Edward Island COVID-19 Proof of Vaccination website .
- Submit your information to get your proof of vaccination.
Prince Edward Island: Proof of vaccination
The COVID-19 proof of vaccination is no longer available in Quebec. To get a copy of your vaccination information, you must submit a request to the Quebec Vaccination Registry .
Quebec: Copy of your vaccination information
- Access your Saskatchewan account .
- Look for your COVID-19 vaccine record. You can print it, download it, store it in the MySaskHealthRecord app or view it on your mobile device.
- You can print it, download it, store it in the MySaskHealthRecord app or view it on your mobile device.
Saskatchewan: Proof of vaccination
- Contact your community health centre.
- Apply online or by phone for your COVID-19 vaccine credential .
Yukon: Get immunized

### Members of the Canadian Armed Forces
Contact your nearest Canadian Armed Forces Health Services Centre for more information on the Canadian COVID-19 proof of vaccination.

## About the Canadian COVID-19 proof of vaccination
In Canada, the COVID-19 proof of vaccination was made available beginning in 2021. It provided those who were vaccinated with a simple, consistent way to view their COVID-19 vaccination history. Although no longer required for travel, you may still be able to obtain a COVID-19 proof of vaccination from your province or territory. It may also be referred to as the 'Canadian' or 'standardized' proof of vaccination.
Once you get your Canadian COVID-19 proof of vaccination, you can access it:
- as a digital file on a mobile device or computer
- on paper by printing the PDF file with the QR code

### Protecting your privacy
The Canadian COVID-19 proof of vaccination shows only a record of your vaccination history. It doesn't expose any of your other health data. The management of your health data is the responsibility of your province, territory or Indigenous organization.
It doesn't contain your health card number, so it can't be used:
- to access other health information
- for other health services
Your proof of vaccination is not digitally connected to any other identification, such as your passport or status card.

### Give feedback about this page
Thank you for your feedback.
